quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: In Hungarian culture, name days are celebrated as much as birthdays! Each day of the year is associated with specific names, and people named Ildikó celebrate their name day on November 17th. It's a tradition where friends and family gather to honor the person, often with flowers, cake, and good wishes.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Harbor Seals
short_read.teaser: Harbor seals, those charming residents of the Strait of Juan de Fuca, have some surprising habits and abilities. Learn how they sleep underwater and navigate the coastal waters you see every day.

medium_read.title: The Ancient Forests of Olympic National Park
medium_read.teaser: Just beyond Port Angeles lie some of the oldest and most magnificent temperate rainforests in North America. Discover the fascinating ecosystem where trees live for centuries and moss blankets create enchanted landscapes.

long_read.title: The Edible Garden at Versailles
long_read.teaser: The legendary kitchen garden at Versailles has been feeding royalty and commoners for centuries with breathtaking beauty and bounty. This fascinating story explores how gardeners transformed vegetables into living art and continue the tradition today.

port_angeles.title: Bald Eagles Along the Waterfront
port_angeles.description: June is a wonderful time to spot bald eagles along Port Angeles Harbor and Ediz Hook. They're often seen fishing in the shallow waters or perched majestically on driftwood logs. Early morning or late afternoon offer the best viewing opportunities.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats approximately ___ times per minute while in flight.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Wonderful! That's right — 1,200 beats per minute! Those tiny hearts work incredibly hard to power their amazing flying abilities.
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! A hummingbird's heart beats about 1,200 times per minute during flight — that's about 20 times per second!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: Those tiny hearts are even faster than you might think — about 1,200 beats per minute! That's what it takes to power their incredible hovering and backward flight.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: Hummingbirds have the fastest metabolism of all animals, which is why they need to eat constantly throughout the day, consuming half their body weight in sugar. Their hearts can slow to 250 beats per minute during rest at night.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Beautiful! Pillangó is indeed the Hungarian word for butterfly — such a lovely, musical word for such a delicate creature!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for butterfly is 'pillangó' — doesn't it sound like it's floating on the breeze? (Virág means flower, madár means bird, and csillag means star.)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is part of the Finno-Ugric language family, making it quite different from other European languages. It's known for its beautiful, flowing sound and poetic compound words.

puzzle_3.question: What special adaptation helps Olympic marmots survive their long winter hibernation?
options: Their heart rate drops to just 3-4 beats per minute
They wake up every week to eat stored food
puzzle_3.correct_response: Exactly right! Olympic marmots slow their hearts to just 3-4 beats per minute during hibernation — an amazing survival trick that lets them sleep through 8 months of winter without eating!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: Olympic marmots have an incredible adaptation — their heart rate drops from 120 beats per minute to just 3-4 beats per minute during hibernation! This allows them to survive 8 months without food in their mountain burrows.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Found only in the Olympic Mountains, these charming animals are one of the most social marmot species. They live in colonies and greet each other by touching noses, earning them the nickname 'whistle pigs' for their warning calls.
puzzle_3.learn_more_label: About Olympic marmots — Olympic National Park

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
